text.title: Staging Smoke Checklist by Surface Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed in real deployments of Staging Smoke Checklist by Surface. Each ties a symptom to a root cause and a concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: 5 surfaces, 30 items; smoke takes 20 minutes; dev skips the bottom half.
antipattern.root-cause: Cap discipline relaxed under feature pressure.
antipattern.fix: Cut to top-3 by impact; remaining surfaces routed to a deeper regression suite.

antipattern.symptom: 'Verify login works' - tester says yep looks fine - nothing actually verified.
antipattern.root-cause: Item phrased as goal not click-expect.
antipattern.fix: Rewrite each item as click-expect with a named target and observable; reject vague verbs without target.

antipattern.symptom: No initials/timestamp/SHA; promotion happens because I think I did it.
antipattern.fix: Add sign-off line; deploy script greps file for the line and aborts if missing or stub.

antipattern.symptom: Item fails; dev ships anyway with will fix in next deploy; bug reaches prod.
antipattern.root-cause: Abort-on-fail rule relaxed under deadline pressure.
antipattern.fix: Treat the gate as binary; revert if needed; file an issue; blameless retro on the override.
